---
title: "Quick Start"
subtitle: "tarnished-ww: joint Bayesian modeling of cases, wastewater, and ED visits"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: true
  warning: false
  message: false
  error: false
  cache: false
jupyter: python3
---

## Overview

This quick start demonstrates how to:

1. Simulate example data
2. Define column mappings
3. Fit a joint Bayesian model (smoke test sampler settings)
4. Generate a forecast

> Note: Sampling can take time depending on your machine. The sampler settings below are intentionally small for a smoke test.

## Quick Start

```{python}
import pandas as pd
from tarnished_ww import fit_joint_model, predict_joint_model
from tarnished_ww import ColumnSpec, PopulationSpec, SamplerSpec
from tarnished_ww.simulate import simulate_synthetic

df, pop = simulate_synthetic(n_regions=2, n_days=60, seed=1)

df.head()
```

```{python}
pop.head()
```

```{python}
cols = ColumnSpec(region="wwtp_id")
pop_cols = PopulationSpec(region="wwtp_id")

cols, pop_cols
```

## Sampler configuration

```{python}
sampler = SamplerSpec(
    draws=100,
    tune=100,
    chains=1,     # <- key change
    cores=1,      # <- key change
    target_accept=0.9,
    random_seed=123,
    extra={
        "progressbar": False,                 # nicer for rendered docs
        "compute_convergence_checks": False,  # fine for smoke test
    }
)

sampler
```

## Fit model

```{python}
res = fit_joint_model(
    df,
    pop,
    diseases=["covid", "rsv", "flua"],
    cols=cols,
    pop_cols=pop_cols,
    sampler=sampler,
)

print("✅ Model built and sampled.")
```

### Forecasting

```{python}
forecast = predict_joint_model(res)
print(forecast["trace"])
```

### Plotting
